mnemonics: Sue U! (Like 'S U' for Super User) - Use 'su' to become the super user.
Please Suck Eggs (P-S-E) - The 'sudo' command requires a Password and executes a Single Elevated command.
keyPoints: The super user (root) has unrestricted access to all commands and files on the system.
The 'su' (substitute user) command is used to switch to another user account, commonly the root user.
The 'sudo' command allows a permitted user to execute a command as the superuser or another user, as specified by the security policy.
commonMistakes: Staying logged in as root for general use, which increases the risk of catastrophic errors.
Using simple or weak passwords for the root account, making the system vulnerable to attack.
Granting unrestricted sudo privileges to standard users, violating the principle of least privilege.
